item.title: Meta Is Building a Cloud Business to Sell Excess AI Compute
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is developing plans for a cloud infrastructure business that will sell access to AI computing power and models, setting up a new vector of competition with industry leaders like Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud.

item.title: S&P Winning Streak for July at Risk With Volatile End to Month
item.description: July is a great time to buy stocks. In fact, it’s been the best month for the S&P 500 Index in the past two decades. Bulls are finding comfort in that history ahead of what stands to be an eventful stretch.

item.title: Software Gains as Guggenheim Says AI No ‘Death Knell’ for Sector
item.description: Software shares rose in premarket trading after Guggenheim upgraded a trio of companies, saying fears that artificial intelligence poses a mortal threat to the sector are overdone.

item.title: US Companies Added 98,000 Jobs in June, ADP Data Show
item.description: US private-sector job creation was solid again in June, capping the best three-month stretch for hiring in more than a year.

item.title: Coffee, Sugar Extend Rally as Adverse Weather Fuels Supply Fears
item.description: Coffee and sugar futures extended gains in New York and London as unfavorable weather in key growing regions heightened supply concerns.

item.title: Taiwan Detains Super Micro Workers in China Chip Smuggling Probe
item.description: Taiwanese prosecutors detained two Super Micro Computer Inc. employees following a raid of the US company’s local offices earlier this week over alleged shipments of Nvidia Corp. chips to China.

item.title: Fed’s Warsh, ECB’s Lagarde Speak at Sintra Conference
item.description: ECB President Christine Lagarde and Kevin Warsh Photographer: Claus Dirk/ECB

item.title: Google Told to Pay Klarna Nearly $2 Billion in Shopping Spat
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google was ordered to pay almost $2 billion to Klarna Group Plc’s Pricerunner unit in a dispute over the search-engine giant’s abuse of power in the market for comparison shopping services.

item.title: Commodities Broker Pulled Back Into Tax Dividend Fraud Suit by UK Court
item.description: ED&F Man Capital Markets Ltd., now MCML Ltd., has been pulled back into a long-running fraud case linked to the Cum-Ex tax dividend scandal after the UK Supreme Court ruled that the Danish tax authority’s lawsuit against the commodities broker can proceed.

item.title: Trump’s White House Takes Aim at EU Emissions Law
item.description: With the US warning it could ship its energy elsewhere, some countries in Europe are urging the bloc to revisit the rules.

item.title: Emerging Market Currencies Erase 2026 Gains as Dollar Advances
item.description: Emerging-market currencies erased gains for this year, with speculation over higher US interest rates sparking a resurgence in the US dollar.

item.title: Bending Spoons IPO Turns Italian College Pals Into Billionaires
item.description: Four former college friends who founded software rollup Bending Spoons SpA have emerged as Italy’s youngest cohort of self-made billionaires.

item.title: Greece’s Jobless Rate Falls to 8.1% in May, Lowest Since 2008
item.description: Greece’s seasonally-adjusted unemployment rate fell to 8.1% in May, the lowest level since November 2008, according to the Hellenic Statistical Authority. That compares with a revised reading of 9.1% in April.

item.title: Oracle Warns Its Splurge on AI Data Centers May Not Pay Off
item.description: The company outlines the risks of its massive cloud build-out to meet AI demand

item.title: AI Productivity Hopes Collide With Job and Market Worries
item.description: OpenAI’s chief economist has soothing words on AI’s employment impact, but job cuts are happening.

item.title: USMCA Heads for Review as Trump Sours on His $2 Trillion Trade Deal
item.description: The 16-year pact has a six-year review clause which the US is expected to invoke

item.title: AI’s Trillion-Dollar Debt Binge Fuels Century-Old Private Market
item.description: A private bond market dating back more than a century is opening a new front in the trillion-dollar AI funding boom, allowing tech borrowers to sell debt directly to deep-pocketed insurance firms.

item.title: Video Search Startup Raises $100 Million From Amazon, VC Funds
item.description: Twelve Labs Inc. is raising $100 million from investors including Amazon.com Inc., NEA Management Co. and Naver Ventures, boosting its efforts to develop artificial intelligence that can quickly search and analyze troves of video.

item.title: Ghana Inflation Rate Rises to Highest Level Since December
item.description: Ghana’s annual inflation rate climbed in June to the highest level so far this year, increasing the likelihood that the central bank will keep interest rates on hold when it meets later this month.

item.title: Wayve Files for Share Sale on London Bourse’s New Private Market
item.description: Autonomous driving software company Wayve Technologies Ltd. has filed to sell shares on the London Stock Exchange’s new Private Securities Market, the first major company to test the novel system.

item.title: AI, Forging New Risks, Isn’t a Market Wizard After All
item.description: More studies are trying to answer the question: what if everyone uses the same AI tool?

item.title: The World’s Second-Biggest Company Isn’t a ‘Rock Star’ Anymore
item.description: Google had a standout year, but now, a shift in the AI trade has it lagging the Nasdaq 100.

item.title: World Bank Backs Nigeria With $1.25 Billion Development Loan
item.description: The World Bank approved $1.25 billion in development financing to help Nigeria spur economic growth and create jobs after the West African nation embarked on reforms praised by the lender.

item.title: Goldman’s Oppenheimer Sees Stock Gains Broadening in Second Half
item.description: Global equities are set to extend their searing second-quarter rally, with earnings growth at technology companies a key driver, according to Peter Oppenheimer at Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.

item.title: ‘Taylor Swift Tax’ Stirs Bad Blood With Rhode Island Homeowners
item.description: Rhode Island’s new tax on second homes is nicknamed for its most famous part-time resident, music superstar Taylor Swift, but the levy will also squeeze owners of saltbox houses on the coast that are much less grand than the singer’s Holiday House mansion.

item.title: Wall Street’s AI Race Is Fueling New Fears of Crowded Trading
item.description: From hedge funds to wealth managers, Wall Street has embraced artificial intelligence in search of an investing edge. Researchers are now asking what happens when more investors turn to similar AI models to find one: buying the same stocks, reacting to the same headlines, and sometimes making the same mistakes.

item.title: Bad Loan Investor APS Eyes €3.3 Billion of Portfolio Deals
item.description: APS Holding is preparing to step up purchases of distressed debt as it sees higher interest rates and real estate stress starting to dent bank balance sheets.

item.title: UK Court Saves Former WH Smith Stores From Brink of Insolvency
item.description: Ailing UK high-street retailer TG Jones Retail Holdings Ltd won court approval for a sweeping financial restructuring that will shutter more than 100 stores and slash rents to stave off insolvency.

item.title: Tech and Finance Sectors Losing 28,000 Jobs Monthly Show AI Impact on Labor
item.description: Whether artificial intelligence will cause mass workforce cuts over time remains up for debate, but it is starting to leave an imprint on US employment data.

item.title: Trump Souring on USMCA Triggers Review of $2 Trillion Trade Deal
item.description: A countdown to the expiry of the US-Mexico-Canada Agreement is set to begin as officials launch a review of the sweeping trade deal that President Donald Trump signed during his first term.
